summary: Drew DeVault's July 2022 status update covers progress across Hare and Helios. For Hare, he introduced a string templating module and a pixel buffer library, laying groundwork for a graphics stack. Helios, his microkernel OS, saw significant systems work including PCI and AHCI drivers, IRQ control for userspace, a DOOM port, and pre-emptive multi-tasking. RISC-V porting also progressed with a working ethernet driver. He outlines next steps around memory capabilities and process spawning.
key_points: Hare's new strings::template module follows the standard library philosophy of simplicity over completeness, avoiding heavyweight dependencies like Jinja2
The pixbuf library begins a planned Hare graphics stack spanning pixel buffers, PNG loading, vector graphics (TinyVG), and Wayland display
Helios gained substantial OS infrastructure: PCI driver, AHCI driver, userspace IRQ control, pre-emptive multitasking, and a DOOM port as proof of concept
The RISC-V port of Helios reached a milestone with a working e1000 ethernet driver capable of sending and receiving ICMP packets
Next priorities are memory capability splitting, capability destruction, and cspace/vspace creation to enable spawning new processes
money_quotes: The templating is pretty simple — as is typical for the standard library — but allows a fairly wide range of formatting options.
It only deals with pixel buffers, handling pixel format conversions and doing small operations like fill and copy.
I also completed IRQ control for userspace, so that userspace device drivers can process interrupts, and used it to write a keyboard driver for a functional DOOM port.
The idle thread was also added, so that all processes can be blocked waiting on interrupts, signals, endpoints, etc.
I also implemented pre-emptive multi-tasking early this month, and the vulcan test suite now has several multi-threaded tests to verify IPC functionality.
key_insight: Hare and Helios are maturing in parallel, with Hare gaining libraries for graphics and templating while Helios reaches key OS milestones like userspace drivers, pre-emptive multitasking, and a working DOOM port.
